observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to regularly
submit the Accomplishment Report of all implemented projects to the
Municipal Accountant’s Office and ensure the timely submission of all
required supporting documents;

1.9 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Identify completed projects still recorded as CIP accounts and
immediately prepare the necessary journal entries to transfer to the
respective PPE accounts ensuring accurate financial reporting; and
b) Conduct monthly reconciliations with implementing
departments/offices to verify the accuracy of recorded transactions
on CIP and PPE proactively addressing any discrepancies and
necessary adjustments in the subsequent periods.

2.9 We recommended and the OMA agreed to:
a) Establish accurate RPT and SET Receivables and corresponding
Deferred Income accounts at the beginning of each period based on
the Certified List of RPT and SET Delinquencies provided by the
PTO;
b) Conduct a thorough reconstruction of missing RPT and SET
Receivables and Deferred Income accounts for CYs 2008 to 2019
(excluding CY 2009) using available supporting documents and
source data from the PTO to reconcile account balances;
c) Ensure that recorded collections are based only on properly
established RPT and SET Receivables; and
d) Institute a regular reconciliation between the Municipality’s and
PTO’s records of RPT and SET Receivables to identify any variances
for timely corrective actions.

3.11 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Prepare adjusting entries to reverse the incorrect capitalization of
infrastructure assets, and reclassify the transactions as Subsidy to
LGUs, aligning with the account classification outlined in Section
330, Volume III of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
b) Ensure that only assets genuinely owned and controlled by the
Municipality are recognized and capitalized, strictly adhering to the
asset recognition criteria stipulated in IPSAS 17.

4.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Prepare and process the necessary adjusting entries to transfer all
unexpended/unobligated CO balances of the LDRRMF from the TF
books back to the GF books;
b) Ensure that only the unutilized balances of QRF and LDRRMF-
MOOE are carried over to the STF under the account “Trust
Liability-DRRM” in the TF books at year-end, in accordance with
Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
c) Treat all unexpended/unobligated LDRRMF-CO balances as
continuing appropriations in the GF books until full completion of
the corresponding projects, consistent with Item 5.1.11 of the
Circular.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Regularly check, monitor and keep the Accounts Payable account updated and adequate; 

Evaluate unclaimed obligations totaling ₱303,052.61 that remained outstanding for two years or more.  In the event that no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by perfected contracts or record on said payables, he may recommend for the reversion of the same pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445 and 5th Indorsement of former COA Commissioner Celso D. Gangan; and

Send notices to individuals who have unclaimed wages, allowances, honorarium, incentive and educational assistance. In the event that the person concerned is not anymore willing to collect their claims, the Municipality may revert the claim to unappropriated surplus.
observations.reason: No action was undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Stop the previous practice of debiting expenses and crediting Grants and Donations in Kind regarding the transfer of completed projects and equipment from Trust Fund to General Fund; and

Discontinue also the prior years’ practice of crediting Grants and Donations in Kind account for the transfer of completed projects from TF to GF books.  Instead, credit directly the transfer of completed projects and equipment to government equity account in order to have a correct and fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Practice is still the same.
Remain outstanding as of CY 2020

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Treasurer:

Intensify collection of delinquent RPT/SET accounts so that the Municipality could generate additional funds for the implementation of priority projects.

Launch effective tax campaign and send notice of delinquency to all past due RPT/SET accounts.
observations.reason: No reason was stated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all dormant accounts shown in the financial statements and support the balances with documentary evidence in order to ascertain their validity, existence and correctness. Further, immediately initiate specific courses of action if correction is necessary.  In the event that the dormant accounts are found to be nonexistent, the said accounts should be elevated to COA for authority/approval of dropping them from the books.
observations.reason: No action was undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the fabrication and installation of conventional sanitary toilets to its proper asset account instead of Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses account to conform to the Revised Chart of Accounts.
observations.reason: No adjustment was made as of CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized LDRRMF-QRF for CYs 2011 and 2012 under the STF to the General Fund thru Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), which shall be made available for other social services upon subsequent enactment by the sanggunian pursuant to Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. 

Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to update the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund and Investment Plan and maximize the utilization of the prior years’ LDRRMF as well as the current year’s fund so it could serve the purpose of equipping the Municipality against the adverse impact of hazards and related disasters.

Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify MDRRM Fund included in the account Due to LGU and ensure proper classification and charges to the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/ Municipal Accountant:

Demand immediate liquidation/settlement of unliquidated cash advances especially those granted December 31, 2011 and below considering that a final notice and demand to all accountable officers must have been issued pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-04.

Send the bill to personnel working in other government agencies demanding liquidation/ settlement/ refund of their unliquidated cash advances. Make representation on this regard to the Head of the Agency concerned and its Accountant.

Conduct periodic evaluation and validation of all unliquidated cash advances.  If necessary, he may request for the write off of dormant cash advances following the procedures of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Refrain from granting cash advance to national agency personnel considering that in case of failure to liquidate cash advance after the prescribed period has elapsed the institution of the withholding of salary could not be effected because they are not employees of the Municipal Government.
observations.reason: Remain outstanding as of CY 2020

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare immediately a list of unexpended PDAF balances which will serve as the basis in the preparation of disbursement vouchers for the refund of the amount to the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 9-81, COA Circular No. 94-013, Executive Order No. 431 and the Supreme Court ruling issued on November 19, 2013.
observations.reason: No action was undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that: 

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the fund transfer to Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines (VLMP) or any implementing agencies is supported with MOA before the release of the fund as required under 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated        June 14, 2012.

The Municipal Accountant records the fund transfer to VLMP as Other Receivables instead of Donation to conform with the Revised Chart of Accounts.
observations.reason: Practice is still the same.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Chairman of the LSB justify the use of the SEF for traveling and registration expenses which are not among the allowable expenses chargeable against the SEF.

The Local School Board allocate the Fund and utilize strictly in accordance with Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 of the Department of Education, DBM and DILG to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.
observations.reason: No reason was stated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Head of the Inventory Committee prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for all unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment and submit the same to the Appraisal Committee so that they could immediately conduct appraisal, determine the appraised value and prepare a report thereon.

After the appraised values are determined, the Inventory Committee immediately file the application for disposal of the unserviceable vehicles, thru the Municipal Mayor, through an IIRUP with the Office of the Audit Team Leader for inspection and determination whether the subject property is with value or without value pursuant to Article 444 of the Rules and Regulations Implementing the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.reason: No reason was stated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer close or transfer the government funds deposited with the PNB to any authorized government depository bank pursuant to the conditions and requirements set forth under DOF Department Circular No. 01-2017 dated May 11, 2017 so that it could comply with the mandate for the sound and efficient management of the financial resources of the government.
observations.reason: No action was undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Project Engineer strictly monitor the project implementation in order to meet the agreed contract time such that the intended recipients or beneficiaries could immediately avail of the benefits derived therefrom.

The Project Engineer also ensure that all winning contractors who will be issued notices of award shall comply with the requirement of Section 39.4 of the said IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer’s Office regularly reconcile their records regarding completed and on-going projects so that the affected accounts are accordingly adjusted and in order to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

It is also suggested that all departments of the Municipality involve in the project implementation must submit their monthly project status report for consolidation and monitoring. 

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor create monitoring team who will take charge in the consolidation of reports of completed and on-going projects, programs and activities of all implementing offices of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Only the Municipal Engineering Office submitted its quarterly status of projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit documents as proof that the Municipality has generated monetary savings as a result of the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts of the employees or group of employees.

Further, the Municipal Mayor explain the granting of cash advance for the achievement incentives prior to the date of resolution approving the latter.

The Calatava PRAISE Committee submit evaluation reports or any documentation recognizing the productivity, creativeness made or ethical behavior of employees or group of employees making them eligible for the PRAISE monetary awards and incentives.

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the amount of incentives to be granted shall not exceed 20% of the monetary savings generated pursuant to the policies of the CSC approved Calatrava PRAISE.

The Municipality submit specific legal basis for the grant of Cash Incentives. Otherwise, we recommend that the said incentives be refunded by the Municipal Government officials and employees for lack of specific legal authority.
observations.reason: No justification was submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management justify the use of the SEF for expenses not in accordance with its primary purpose. Should justification be unacceptable, the paid expenses of ₱350,632.26 be disallowed in audit

The Local School Board allocate the Fund SEF and utilized strictly in accordance with Article 363(c) of the IRR of R.A. 7160 and the provisions of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A and 01-B series of 1998,2000 and 2001, respectively to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.
observations.reason: No reason was given/stated.

observations.recommendation:
The MPD Officer and the Municipal Engineer including officials involved in the project implementation adhere to the provisions of the DILG & DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 with regard to the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure that all projects to be funded shall partake the nature of investment and capital expenditures.

The Municipality Mayor manage the resources of the government through proper disposition.

The Project Engineer submit duly received distribution lists and proof that the materials were properly utilized for its intended purpose. Otherwise, he shall be held liable for materials distributed not duly accounted.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee composed of the representative from the Mayor’s Office and the Accounting to undertake the physical inventory of the supplies and properties and submit an inventory report which is duly reconciled with the Accounting records.

Further, it is reminded that failure to comply with the submission of reports as cited in Section 122 of PD 1445 will automatically cause the suspension of payment of salaries of concerned officials or shall be punished under Section 128 of PD 1445.

Management should explain the use of the SEF for expenses not in accordance with its purpose. Should justification be unacceptable, the paid expenses of ₱251,188.80 shall be disallowed.
Funds allocated for SEF should be budgeted and utilized strictly in accordance with Article 363(c) of the IRR of R.A. 7160 and the provisions of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A and 01-B series of 1998,2000 and 2001, respectively to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommend that the management comply with the guidelines on the grant of cash advance for intelligence fund as set forth in Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Social Welfare Officer set a clear-cut guidelines or policies in the implementation of Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation (AICS) in conformity with the guidelines provided by the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD). 

We recommend also that grant of financial assistance should be supported with complete and appropriate documentation. 

Further, we recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare Office should conduct a thorough assessment on the qualifications of the individuals seeking for financial assistance before making any recommendation in order to safeguard government funds against wastage, illegal or improper disposition.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request for the authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: The amount still remained in the books as at December 31, 2020 and still classified as current assets. No updates relative to the tracing of the whereabouts of the persons accountable were submitted as of date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Social Welfare Office or other offices in-charge in the distribution to submit the distribution lists of welfare goods given to the beneficiaries-recipients for validation/support of the funds disbursed and distributed;
Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to ascertain that procurement of supplies and other inventory items should be taken up as inventory; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the requesting office to fill out the RIS for the issuance of inventory items to the end-users/offices, while release of welfare good/medicines to beneficiaries-recipients should be supported with properly prepared distribution list.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the BAC and end-user/implementing office to undertake only procurement activities in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan and revised/update the plan if necessary to include items not considered in the original plan and indicate in the APP the mode of procurement to be adopted pursuant to RA No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR); and
Discontinue the practice of paying huge amounts of disbursements by cash and adhere to the general principle that all disbursement must be paid by check pursuant the General Principle No. 2 prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 9, COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 to protect government funds against unauthorized use or misappropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the IT specialist hired or personnel in charge to comply with the posting requirements set forth by the LBC Circular in order to promote accountability and transparency in the utilization and disbursement of grants.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to require the supplier to submit an Omnibus Sworn Statement except for procurement project with ABC of below ₱50,000.00 on subsequent transactions pursuant to the requirement of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR and the GPPB.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the BAC to update its Annual Procurement Plan (APP), prepare the Procurement Management Reports (PMRs), post to the Municipality’s official website and submit to the GPPB the foregoing reports in consonance with GPPB Circular 02-2020 dated May 20, 2020 in order to promote transparency and accountability in the procurement activities; and
Direct the end user or implementing office and BAC to undertake changes to the individual PPMPs and consolidated APP every six months or as often as may be required by the HoPE and submit the same to the GPPB within the prescribed period set forth by the latter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the accounting guidelines for LDRRMF set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, reclassify the remaining balances of unexpended Quick Response Fund and Mitigation Fund-unexpended MOOE under Trust Fund and prepare necessary adjustment as follows:
Due to LGUs			₱16,188,529.39
Trust Liabilities-DRRMF		₱16,188,529.39

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor will designate new members of the Inventory Committee who will see to it that requirements relative to the conduct of complete physical count of all the inventories and property, plant and equipment of the Municipality at year-end are duly complied with to determine the validity, reliability and existence of its inventories and properties.  The committee shall reconcile the results of the count with the property records and accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.   Coordinated efforts of the employees should be encouraged to be able to submit the required reports.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to:

furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Contract of Agreement and its attachments within five working days from the execution thereof as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 23, 2009; and

observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor will direct the:

Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to (i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format prescribed in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; (ii) submit the Municipality’s Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with Item No. 5.1.5 of the same circular; (iii) properly plan the programs, projects and activities to be undertaken, fast track their implementation and optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the Municipality in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters; (iv) ensure that all fuel withdrawals shall be properly controlled, monitored and accounted for through the use of Driver’s Trip Tickets indicating the purpose and destination of the vehicles duly approved by authorized official; and (v) submit to the auditor, a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of FOL products for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness and validity of fuel withdrawals;

The BAC to (i) strictly adhere to the requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the procurement of goods and services charged against the LDRRMF; and (ii) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Purchase Order and its attachments within five working days from the issuance thereof as required under Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and

The Municipal Accountant to (i) account inventory items to their corresponding inventory accounts and record the corresponding expense only upon issuance/consumption thereof; (ii) review the regularity and completeness of supporting documents of disbursement vouchers prescribed under Section 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 - 001 before processing claims for payment; and (iii) adhere strictly with the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the management agreed that:

The Municipal Mayor along with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in planning the programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and their implementation. 

For the delay implementation of projects due to failed bidding, refer to Sections 35.2 and 35.3 of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 which provides that the BAC shall conduct a mandatory review and evaluation of the terms, conditions and specifications in the bidding documents, including cost estimates. Based on its findings, the BAC shall revise the terms, conditions, and specifications, and if necessary, adjust the ABC, subject to the required approvals, and conduct a re-bidding with re-advertisement and/or posting.

If bidding could not be ultimately pursued and the Municipality has no other option but to undertake the project by administration, ensure that the Revised Guidelines for the Implementation of Infrastructure Projects by Administration provided under Appendix 1 of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 are strictly complied with.

Relative to the immediately preceding recommendation, wherein delay could still be noted considering that i) few workers are willing to undertake the project as the wage rate being offered by the Municipality is lower compared to the rate offered by private contractors; and ii) the mandatory deduction of applicable taxes, the Municipal Mayor in coordination with other concerned offices pursue its plan of  modifying the existing wage rate being implemented for the purpose of ensuring that delivery of public services will not be impaired.  It is to be emphasized, however, that the financial capability of the Municipality and compliance with budgetary requirements/limitations provided under existing laws, rules and regulations be considered.

For projects which were delayed due to problem on road right of way or the site was not viable for the construction of water tank, the Municipal Mayor, henceforth, require the Municipal Engineer to conduct applicable detailed engineering activities to serve as basis of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in properly evaluating the technical viability of proposed projects before inclusion in the list of priority projects to be included in the 20% DF.  In this way, projects with issues, not feasible or has a remote possibility of being implemented may not be considered during the budget year;

The BAC i) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Purchase Order and its attachments within five working days from the issuance as required under Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; ii) observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring; and iii) validate the mathematical computations of supporting documents to avoid discrepancies thereof;

The concerned officials refer to Item 6.1, Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 regarding the imposition of the 10% retention money; and

The Municipal Accountant see to it that all the necessary documentary requirements attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before processing the claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirement of the perpetual inventory system wherein regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place except those purchased out of petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use and not for stock. Such case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate expense accounts. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall regularly prepare and submit accurate and complete Summary of Supplies and Materials Issue (SSMI) with supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) pursuant to Section 121 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to the Accounting Unit for proper recording/posting of issuance in the books of accounts and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).

On the other hand, we recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare JEVs to adjust/record issuances on the affected accounts and coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure reconciliation of their inventory records, also in respect of physical inventory taking.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all other concerned officials and employees adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1 in planning and implementing programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents. Likewise, we recommended that the management should facilitate adequate planning and budgeting for development projects and refrain lump sum/generic term appropriation by identifying the individual PAPs inputted in the AIP.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for 20% development fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to establish a clear-cut guidelines, policies and procedures on the selection and grant of cash assistance aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014.




We recommended that the Municipal Mayor leave the determination of the type, kind and amount of cash assistance to be given to the individuals based on the evaluation of the MSWDO in compliance with the aforementioned guideline.




We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that grant of cash assistance are supported with the required documentary requirements prior to processing for payment. Submit the required lacking documents to prove the validity, regularity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipality observe the limit of granting cash assistance directly to the recipients as required in the afore-cited memorandum circular.

We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere strictly to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all other concerned officials and employees adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 in planning and implementing programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to observe timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget to DILG Regional Office for review and approval in compliance with PCW-DILG-DNM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.




We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) facilitate the updating of its existing GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAsbe incorporated in the GPB are responsive to address the same.



We recommended that the GFPS strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under JMC No. 2013-01 and to conduct regular coordination with the implementing office toensure that plans are undertaken within the target period. Likewise,proper identification andprioritization of PPAs that would effectively address gender issues and concerns be conducted and ensure its inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget to be implemented during the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

strictly observe the existing rules and regulations governing collections and deposits of government funds to avoid the possible loss and misuse of government funds. 

observe the proper issuance of official receipts. See to it that a set of accountable form is entirely used up before using/issuing another set of official receipt for proper accounting and monitoring.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request for the authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off dormant receivable accounts pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation the Municipal Accountant carefully analyze and review the accounting entries made on the accounts affected, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect appropriate adjustments thereof to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the agency’s financialposition. Submit the Journal Entry Voucher to support the adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents to establish the validity of year-end obligations. Also, review and analyze accounts payable in prior years to determine the validity of claims and take-up the required adjustments, where necessary.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with paragraph e, Section 4 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 10 of the same Manual, Volume II.

We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above, and submit lacking documents to prove the contract’s validity, accuracy, regularity and propriety.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to observe the procurement rules and timelines required under RA No. 9184 and require the winning bidders to post the required performance security and enter into contract with the PE or the municipality within 10 calendar days from receipt by the winning bidders of the Notices of Award (NOA) pursuant to       Sections 37.2.1 and 39.1 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 since the said stages of procurement process are mandatory in nature except only for justifiable cause pursuant to Section 65.1 (b) of the IRR of RA No. 9184; and

Require the BAC to ensure that the NOA is properly received by the winning bidder by indicating the date of receipt. The winning bidder shall post the performance security and enter into contract within 10 calendar days from receipt of NOA.

We recommended that the Project Engineer ensure that all winning contractors comply with the requirement of Section 39.4 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 so that all completed projects are covered by performance security during the defects liability period to guarantee the faithful performance by the contractors to undertake repair works in case defects occur within one year from project completion up to final acceptance.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer include the installation of signboards/ billboards at the project sites of all infrastructure projects regardless of funding source as pay item in the program of works which will then be included in the infrastructure contract.

We recommended that the Municipality consider the options suggested in        Item 3.4 of Local Budget Circular No. 100 considering that the Water Works income is not sufficient to finance its overall operation.

We recommended that the Municipal Government include in its Solid Waste Management Plan the identification of a proposed disposal sites or develop strategies for the efficient disposal of waste through existing disposal facilities and strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumpsites pursuant to Section 2, Rule XVIII of RA No. 9003.
observations.reason: A retaining wall was constructed on slope portion of the dumpsite to prevent scattering of solid wastes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) monitor and evaluate the implementation of the priority development projects and ensure that these projects are implemented based on the timelines and must see to it that the funds are utilized in accordance with the purpose for which it was appropriated; and

The MDC ascertain that all project proposals for inclusion in the 20% Development Plan are properly scrutinized/evaluated and prioritize execution of development projects that are readily implementable without unresolved issues to prevent further delays in the implementation and completion of these projects, thereby attaining its objectives of providing desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes pursuant to JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.reason: Only 5 out of 28 unimplemented projects were completed based on the report submitted by the MPDO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer submit a copy of the computer-generated collectibles at the beginning of the year as basis of the Municipal Accountant for the yearly recognition of its RPT/SET Receivables and the corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income in the books pursuant to Section 20 of MNGAS, Volume I.

The Municipal Treasurer develop strategies and exert extra effort to collect long overdue RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant review the Due to NGAs account specifically transfer of funds with dormant balances.  Further, we recommend that any unutilized balances must be returned to the Source Agency or may be utilized for other purposes depending on the conditions stated in the Memorandum of Agreement.

In the event that the funding source agency could not be determined, the Municipal Accountant request for write off pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: Some unutilized balances were returned to the Source Agency.
The projects were already completed; however, submission of actual contract between the Municipality and HELM Construction and Supplies was not complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer release the RPT share of each barangay directly to the Barangay Treasurer in compliance with Section 271 (d) of Republic Act 7160 instead of transferring the amount to the Trust Fund before releasing the same to the respective barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Engineer expedite the implementation of the two BuB projects with funding from the National Government Agencies so the constituents could fully enjoy the benefits that can be derived from these projects.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts pursuant to Item 3.20 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to verify the correctness and validity of recorded entries in the general ledger and bank statements on time.

Request the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the general ledger balances of Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Accounts and Cash Book balances of each bank account at least quarterly.
observations.reason: No bank reconciliation statement was prepared for non-moving account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Supply Officer identify the properties which are not provided with depreciation.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant immediately compute for the depreciation of each item identified pursuant to COA Circular No.    2004-005 dated August 9, 2004 and accordingly adjust the useful lives of these assets and charge appropriately their value to the remaining useful lives in order to come up with the correct and fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

Also, the Municipal Accountant should disclose the amount of unidentified properties in the Notes to FS so that the PPE account is fairly presented in the FS.

We recommended that:

The Project Engineer strictly monitor the project implementation in order to meet the agreed contract time such that the intended recipients or beneficiaries could immediately avail of the benefits derived from such projects.

The Project Engineer also ensure that all winning contractors who would be issued notices of award shall comply with the requirement of Section 39.4 of the said IRR.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor and the MPD Coordinator immediately implement projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund to achieve the socio-economic and environmental management outcomes. 
The MDC involved in the planning process ensure that development projects to be included in the Annual Investment Plan are properly evaluated and adequately planned to fast track project implementation.

The MPD Coordinator prepare and submit not only the current year Report of the 20% component of the IRA Utilization but also the status of implementation of all projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund.

The Municipal Officials concerned provide justification or explanation for charging the Improvement of Guesthouse, Senior Citizen Building and Pan-ad Booth against the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Some PYs projects remained not yet fully implemented as of CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Government strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumpsites pursuant to Section 2, Rule XVIII of Republic Act 9003 until such time that a sanitary land fill is constructed.

The Municipal Government expedite the construction of landfill so it could comply with the requirements of R.A. 9003 in its ecological solid waste management.
observations.reason: A retaining wall was constructed on slope portion of the dumpsite to prevent scattering of solid wastes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Memorandum of Agreement for fund transfer to barangay includes provision/obligation of the recipient LGUs to submit liquidation reports after the purpose of the fund transfer has been served.

The proponent barangay prepares the Project Proposal for approval of the Municipal Mayor. Once it is approved, the Municipality will then prepare the MOA.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant records the fund transfer to barangay as Due from LGUs instead of Subsidy to LGUs to conform to the Revised Chart of Accounts. In addition, he must require submission of liquidation reports for the financial assistance given or require the return or refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012 and ensure that previous fund transfer is liquidated after the purpose has been accomplished.

The Monitoring Committee conduct constant monitoring of project implementation to ascertain that the funds granted as financial assistance is utilized for its intended purpose.
observations.reason: Project Proposal of the proponent barangay and the Memorandum of Agreement were still prepared simultaneously.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. 
	
We recommend also that the Municipal Accountant establish the Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year based on Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card or on a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

Further, we recommend that the Accounting Office observe the proper accounting method and accounting entries in recognizing and recording real property taxes.

We recommend that the Municipality strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act 9003 and also allocate sufficient funds to address the existing problems like the lack of manpower and equipments.

Also, we recommend that the management prioritize the search for a suitable sanitary landfill in order to safeguard the life of its constituents and the environment as well.
observations.reason: A retaining wall was constructed on slope portion of the dumpsite to prevent scattering of solid wastes.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend also that the GAD Focal Person adhere to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 on the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget.

Further, we recommend that the GAD Focal Person submit the Annual GAD Plan and Budget to the agencies requiring them.
We recommend that all procurements of the Municipality must be made in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the Revised IRR of Republic Act 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommend that Municipal Accountant submit the required reports to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period as required by existing rules & regulations. Failure to comply with the regulations may cause the suspension of payments of salaries of responsible personnel.

Further, it is reminded that failure to comply with the submission of reports as cited in Section 122 of PD 1445 will automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they have complied with the requirements of the Commission or shall be punished under Section 128 of PD 1445 by a fine not exceeding one thousand pesos or by imprisonment not exceeding six (6) months, or both such fine and imprisonment in the discretion of the court.
observations.reason: Submission was made annually.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts to verify the correctness and validity of recorded entries in the general ledger and bank statements on time.

Require also the Municipal Treasurer to cooperate with Municipal Accountant in the preparation of bank reconciliation statements to reconcile the general ledger and Cash Book balances every end of the month.
observations.reason: No bank reconciliation statement was prepared for non-moving accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 regarding the documentary requirements for procurement. It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the attached supporting documents are complete before affixing his signature.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should require the officials concerned to prepare the guidelines or policies in the implementation of the Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation (AICS) and should conform to the guidelines provided by the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD).

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submits the monthly Consolidated Report on the Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAFs) from January 2010 to July 2013 to the Audit Team for verification and analysis.
observations.reason: Submission was made upon request.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Officer-in-charge of the Municipal Accountant’s Office should exert extra efforts and initiate action plan for the immediate submission of the bank reconciliation statements from as of January 31, 2010 to May 31, 2013 and the subsequent periods.
observations.reason: No bank reconciliation statement was prepared for non-moving account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that tangible items acquired with estimated useful life of more than one year shall be recorded as inventories and outright expense upon issuance in accordance with Sec. 2.2.1 of COA Circular 2005-002 in order to reflect proper account classification in the financial statements.

Require the persons liable to refund for the excess travel claims.       Re-orient concerned personnel and personnel in charge of the review of travel claims regarding the provisions of Section 4 of Executive No. 298 dated March 23, 2004.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor will designate new members of the Inventory Committee who will see to it that requirements relative to the conduct of complete physical count of all the inventories and property, plant and equipment of the Municipality at year-end are duly complied with to determine the validity, reliability and existence of its inventories and properties.  The committee shall reconcile the results of the count with the property records and accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.   Coordinated efforts of the employees should be encouraged to be able to submit the required reports.
observations.managementAction: Executive Order No. 3 S. 2021 re-organizing the Municipal Inventory Committee was issued and said Committee have already planned and discussed the activities based on procedural guidelines to execute the inventory taking efficiently and accurately.  Preparation of significant documents prior to the actual inventory is on-going.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committed is still on the process of conducting the physical inventory of properties.  No inventory reports were prepared and submitted to the Audit Team yet.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to:

furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Contract of Agreement and its attachments within five working days from the execution thereof as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 23, 2009; and

observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring.
observations.managementAction: There were copies of contract agreements and purchase orders which were furnished to the Audit Team within five days from execution/ issuance thereof.
observations.reason: Most of the BAC members, its Secretariat and TWG were one –man team in their regular offices thus, there were times when they inadvertently fail to comply with some of the requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  For specific lapses noted in this audit observation, reasons for failure to comply were presented under the management’s comments portion of observation no. 3 of this report.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor designated SB Marnal Mores as Vice Chairman of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and Mr. Edward Russell F. Bagnate as the Focal Person since he is the Sanitary Inspector.
observations.reason:
The Municipality has not yet established a sanitary landfill.  Recently, a national directive was already issued to the Municipality for the closure of their existing dump site, hence, as of the moment the Municipality has temporarily stopped the collection of garbage from household and various establishments. 

Other audit recommendations were not also complied by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor will direct the:

Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to (i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format prescribed in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; (ii) submit the Municipality’s Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with Item No. 5.1.5 of the same circular; (iii) properly plan the programs, projects and activities to be undertaken, fast track their implementation and optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the Municipality in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters; (iv) ensure that all fuel withdrawals shall be properly controlled, monitored and accounted for through the use of Driver’s Trip Tickets indicating the purpose and destination of the vehicles duly approved by authorized official; and (v) submit to the auditor, a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of FOL products for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness and validity of fuel withdrawals;

The BAC to (i) strictly adhere to the requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the procurement of goods and services charged against the LDRRMF; and (ii) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Purchase Order and its attachments within five working days from the issuance thereof as required under Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and

The Municipal Accountant to (i) account inventory items to their corresponding inventory accounts and record the corresponding expense only upon issuance/consumption thereof; (ii) review the regularity and completeness of supporting documents of disbursement vouchers prescribed under Section 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012 - 001 before processing claims for payment; and (iii) adhere strictly with the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction:
The LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 was already prepared in accordance with the required format provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, however, they were not still able to include the appropriations funded out of the special trust fund due to unreconciled balances between the MDRRMC’s monitoring report and accounting records.

Costs of the LDRRM projects were classified to its appropriate expense class.
observations.reason:
On the utilization rate of the LDRRMF for CY 2020, the MDRRMO explained that delays were due to several community quarantine imposed because of the COVID-19 pandemic. Implementation/ procurement of several LDRRM supplies and equipment were not pushed through because there was no available local supplier and in fear of contracting COVID-19 from procuring these supplies and equipment outside the province.

As to the delays incurred by the Municipality in the submission of Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management, reconciliation with the accounting records has to be made yet.

As to the recording of procurement of fuel for use of Disaster Vehicle directly to expense account, the Municipal Accountant explained that those fuel has already been liquidated and were also supported with trip tickets but were not just submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that:

The Municipal Mayor along with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in planning the programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and their implementation. 

For the delay implementation of projects due to failed bidding, refer to Sections 35.2 and 35.3 of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 which provides that the BAC shall conduct a mandatory review and evaluation of the terms, conditions and specifications in the bidding documents, including cost estimates. Based on its findings, the BAC shall revise the terms, conditions, and specifications, and if necessary, adjust the ABC, subject to the required approvals, and conduct a re-bidding with re-advertisement and/or posting.

If bidding could not be ultimately pursued and the Municipality has no other option but to undertake the project by administration, ensure that the Revised Guidelines for the Implementation of Infrastructure Projects by Administration provided under Appendix 1 of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 are strictly complied with.

Relative to the immediately preceding recommendation, wherein delay could still be noted considering that i) few workers are willing to undertake the project as the wage rate being offered by the Municipality is lower compared to the rate offered by private contractors; and ii) the mandatory deduction of applicable taxes, the Municipal Mayor in coordination with other concerned offices pursue its plan of  modifying the existing wage rate being implemented for the purpose of ensuring that delivery of public services will not be impaired.  It is to be emphasized, however, that the financial capability of the Municipality and compliance with budgetary requirements/limitations provided under existing laws, rules and regulations be considered.

For projects which were delayed due to problem on road right of way or the site was not viable for the construction of water tank, the Municipal Mayor, henceforth, require the Municipal Engineer to conduct applicable detailed engineering activities to serve as basis of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in properly evaluating the technical viability of proposed projects before inclusion in the list of priority projects to be included in the 20% DF.  In this way, projects with issues, not feasible or has a remote possibility of being implemented may not be considered during the budget year;

The BAC i) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Purchase Order and its attachments within five working days from the issuance as required under Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; ii) observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring; and iii) validate the mathematical computations of supporting documents to avoid discrepancies thereof;

The concerned officials refer to Item 6.1, Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 regarding the imposition of the 10% retention money; and

The Municipal Accountant see to it that all the necessary documentary requirements attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before processing the claim.
observations.managementAction: During the year, 13 development projects were implemented and eight were realigned/ reprogrammed to COVID-19 priority projects in accordance with the DILG-DBM JMC No. 01.
observations.reason:
Two projects from current and continuing appropriations were not implemented during the year due to the following:

Engineering Office was not able to study the sustainability of the identified water source due to rainy season all throughout the year; and
On-going preparation of plans and program of works and that the technical person commissioned to prepare the plans and technical specifications was not able to visit the site due to COVID-19 restrictions. 

Other deficiencies were still noted and discussed under observation no. 7 of this report.

On the other hand, we recommended that Municipal Accountant prepare JEVs to adjust/record issuances on the affected accounts and coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure reconciliation of their inventory records, also in respect of physical inventory taking.

observations.recommendation:

strictly observe the existing rules and regulations governing collections and deposits of government funds to avoid the possible loss and misuse of government funds. 

observe the proper issuance of official receipts. See to it that a set of accountable form is entirely used up before using/issuing another set of official receipt for proper accounting and monitoring.

Submit the Journal Entry Voucher to support the adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents to establish the validity of year-end obligations. Also, review and analyze accounts payable in prior years to determine the validity of claims and take-up the required adjustments, where necessary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer submit a copy of the computer-generated collectibles at the beginning of the year as basis of the Municipal Accountant for the yearly recognition of its RPT/SET Receivables and the corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income in the books pursuant to Section 20 of MNGAS, Volume I.

The Municipal Treasurer develop strategies and exert extra effort to collect long overdue RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer:

Intensify collection of delinquent RPT/SET accounts so that the Municipality could generate additional funds for the implementation of priority projects.


Launch effective tax campaign and send notice of delinquency to all past due RPT/SET accounts.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant review the Due to NGAs account specifically transfer of funds with dormant balances.  Further, we recommend that any unutilized balances must be returned to the Source Agency or may be utilized for other purposes depending on the conditions stated in the Memorandum of Agreement.

In the event that the funding source agency could not be determined, the Municipal Accountant request for write off pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all dormant accounts shown in the financial statements and support the balances with documentary evidence in order to ascertain their validity, existence and correctness. Further, immediately initiate specific courses of action if correction is necessary.  In the event that the dormant accounts are found to be nonexistent, the said accounts should be elevated to COA for authority/approval of dropping them from the books.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts pursuant to Item 3.20 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to verify the correctness and validity of recorded entries in the general ledger and bank statements on time.

Request the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the general ledger balances of Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Accounts and Cash Book balances of each bank account at least quarterly.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the fabrication and installation of conventional sanitary toilets to its proper asset account instead of Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses account to conform to the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized LDRRMF-QRF for CYs 2011 and 2012 under the STF to the General Fund thru Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), which shall be made available for other social services upon subsequent enactment by the sanggunian pursuant to Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. 

Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to update the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund and Investment Plan and maximize the utilization of the prior years’ LDRRMF as well as the current year’s fund so it could serve the purpose of equipping the Municipality against the adverse impact of hazards and related disasters.

Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify MDRRM Fund included in the account Due to LGU and ensure proper classification and charges to the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/ Municipal Accountant:

Demand immediate liquidation/settlement of unliquidated cash advances especially those granted December 31, 2011 and below considering that a final notice and demand to all accountable officers must have been issued pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-04.

Send the bill to personnel working in other government agencies demanding liquidation/ settlement/ refund of their unliquidated cash advances. Make representation on this regard to the Head of the Agency concerned and its Accountant.

Conduct periodic evaluation and validation of all unliquidated cash advances.  If necessary, he may request for the write off of dormant cash advances following the procedures of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Refrain from granting cash advance to national agency personnel considering that in case of failure to liquidate cash advance after the prescribed period has elapsed the institution of the withholding of salary could not be effected because they are not employees of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Supply Officer identify the properties which are not provided with depreciation.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant immediately compute for the depreciation of each item identified pursuant to COA Circular No.    2004-005 dated August 9, 2004 and accordingly adjust the useful lives of these assets and charge appropriately their value to the remaining useful lives in order to come up with the correct and fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

Also, the Municipal Accountant should disclose the amount of unidentified properties in the Notes to FS so that the PPE account is fairly presented in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare immediately a list of unexpended PDAF balances which will serve as the basis in the preparation of disbursement vouchers for the refund of the amount to the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 9-81, COA Circular No. 94-013, Executive Order No. 431 and the Supreme Court ruling issued on November 19, 2013.

We recommended that: 

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the fund transfer to Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines (VLMP) or any implementing agencies is supported with MOA before the release of the fund as required under 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated        June 14, 2012.

The Municipal Accountant records the fund transfer to VLMP as Other Receivables instead of Donation to conform with the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that:

The Chairman of the LSB justify the use of the SEF for traveling and registration expenses which are not among the allowable expenses chargeable against the SEF.

The Local School Board allocate the Fund and utilize strictly in accordance with Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 of the Department of Education, DBM and DILG to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommended that:

The Head of the Inventory Committee prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for all unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment and submit the same to the Appraisal Committee so that they could immediately conduct appraisal, determine the appraised value and prepare a report thereon.

After the appraised values are determined, the Inventory Committee immediately file the application for disposal of the unserviceable vehicles, thru the Municipal Mayor, through an IIRUP with the Office of the Audit Team Leader for inspection and determination whether the subject property is with value or without value pursuant to Article 444 of the Rules and Regulations Implementing the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer close or transfer the government funds deposited with the PNB to any authorized government depository bank pursuant to the conditions and requirements set forth under DOF Department Circular No. 01-2017 dated May 11, 2017 so that it could comply with the mandate for the sound and efficient management of the financial resources of the government.

We recommended that:

The Project Engineer strictly monitor the project implementation in order to meet the agreed contract time such that the intended recipients or beneficiaries could immediately avail of the benefits derived therefrom.


The Project Engineer also ensure that all winning contractors who will be issued notices of award shall comply with the requirement of Section 39.4 of the said IRR.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor and the MPD Coordinator immediately implement projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund to achieve the socio-economic and environmental management outcomes. 

The MDC involved in the planning process ensure that development projects to be included in the Annual Investment Plan are properly evaluated and adequately planned to fast track project implementation.

The MPD Coordinator prepare and submit not only the current year Report of the 20% component of the IRA Utilization but also the status of implementation of all projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund.

The Municipal Officials concerned provide justification or explanation for charging the Improvement of Guesthouse, Senior Citizen Building and Pan-ad Booth against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Government strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumpsites pursuant to Section 2, Rule XVIII of Republic Act 9003 until such time that a sanitary land fill is constructed.

The Municipal Government expedite the construction of landfill so it could comply with the requirements of R.A. 9003 in its ecological solid waste management.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer’s Office regularly reconcile their records regarding completed and on-going projects so that the affected accounts are accordingly adjusted and in order to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

It is also suggested that all departments of the Municipality involve in the project implementation must submit their monthly project status report for consolidation and monitoring. 

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor create monitoring team who will take charge in the consolidation of reports of completed and on-going projects, programs and activities of all implementing offices of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit documents as proof that the Municipality has generated monetary savings as a result of the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts of the employees or group of employees.

Further, the Municipal Mayor explain the granting of cash advance for the achievement incentives prior to the date of resolution approving the latter.

The Calatava PRAISE Committee submit evaluation reports or any documentation recognizing the productivity, creativeness made or ethical behavior of employees or group of employees making them eligible for the PRAISE monetary awards and incentives.

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the amount of incentives to be granted shall not exceed 20% of the monetary savings generated pursuant to the policies of the CSC approved Calatrava PRAISE.
The Municipality submit specific legal basis for the grant of Cash Incentives. Otherwise, we recommend that the said incentives be refunded by the Municipal Government officials and employees for lack of specific legal authority.

We recommended that the Memorandum of Agreement for fund transfer to barangay includes provision/obligation of the recipient LGUs to submit liquidation reports after the purpose of the fund transfer has been served.

Management’s Comments

           The Municipal Mayor  commented  that  it  was  Mr.  John  Arvill
      Rabino, MDRRMO and Acting MENRO, who  was  assigned  to  oversee  the
      activities  relative  to  the  implementation  of  the  solid   waste
      management program of the Municipality.   But  Mr.  Rabino  explained
      that he could no longer attend to the requirements of R.A.  No.  9003
      considering also  the  bulk  of  requirements  relative  to  disaster
      management.  The Municipal Mayor then appointed Mr.  Russel  Bagnate,
      the duly designated SWM Focal person, to assume  the  responsibility.
      The latter, although a little bit hesitant, accepted the challenge.


      Auditor’s Rejoinder

                The Municipal Mayor is reminded that the SWM  Focal  Person
      who was  designated  to  oversee  and  monitor  compliance  with  the
      provisions of R.A. No. 9003 be required to undergo proper orientation
      since it is very important for him to be  aware  of  his  duties  and
      responsibilities to ensure smooth implementation of the  solid  waste
      management programs.

    b.  The LGU is still operating a controlled dump site for  the  disposal
      of its solid wastes.


           Interview with the OIC-MENRO disclosed that as of CY  2019,  the
      Municipality  is  continuously  operating  a  controlled  dump   site
      situated in Barangay Linao as its final disposal facility  for  solid
      wastes despite the provision in Section 37  of  R.A.  No.  9003  that
      controlled dumpsite shall no longer be allowed five  years  following
      the effectivity of the Act.  Further, DENR Administrative  Order  No.
      09 dated September 14, 2006 sets out the general  guidelines  in  the
      Closure and Rehabilitation of  Open  Dumpsites  and  Controlled  Dump
      Facilities and also provides that all open dumps and controlled  dump
      facilities should have been closed and phased out as of February  16,
      2006.


           He also explained that the Municipality still was  not  able  to
      locate a suitable sanitary landfill that matches the minimum criteria
      for siting of  sanitary  landfill  provided  in  Section  40  of  the
      Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 thus, the  Municipality
      still opted to operate a controlled dump site  facility  despite  the
      provisions cited above.




      Management’s Comments

           The Municipal Mayor informed the  Audit  Team  that  aside  from
      financial constraints, the dilemma that  all  municipalities  in  the
      province, even in  the  whole  country,  are  facing  is  locating  a
      suitable sanitary landfill that will match the minimum  criteria  for
      siting of sanitary landfill provided in Section 40 of the  Ecological
      Solid Waste  Management  Act  of  2000.   They  even  considered  the
      possibility of teaming up or  clustering  with  other  municipalities
      just like what is being practiced in the  City  of  Muntinlupa  which
      according to the Municipal Mayor, said Municipality has  been  paying
      the required fees to the Municipality of Montalban, Rizal for them to
      be able to dump their garbage in the said area.


           She also stressed  that,  despite  the  absence  of  a  sanitary
      landfill, they are putting their best efforts in properly maintaining
      their existing dumpsite. She assured  the  Audit  Team  that  control
      measures are being put in place to avert potential negative impact in
      the environment such as the  construction  of  slope  protection  and
      providing soil cover as often as needed  so  as  not  to  expose  the
      wastes being dumped there.  They were also very confident  to  inform
      the Audit Team that segregation at source is being practiced  by  the
      households  as  their  Materials   Recovery   Facility   is   already
      operational.  The MDRRMO added that he was designated as OIC MENRO as
      this is one of the  requirements  for  the  Municipality  to  receive
      assistance from the EMB for the proper  implementation  of  it  solid
      waste management program.


      Auditor’s Rejoinder

            The Audit Team commends the best efforts being put  in  by  the
      Municipality in managing their wastes, however, they should not  stop
      from taking measures to ensure compliance with the provisions of R.A.
      No. 9003 especially in the establishment of the sanitary landfill.


            The deficiencies noted above may result in conditions which  may
      be prejudicial to the interest of the constituents on the  aspect  of
      public health and environmental hazards.  Hence, the total protection
      of the environment and the general welfare  of  the  constituents  in
      harmony with economic development may not be substantially attained.


         In view of the foregoing  observations,  we  recommended  and  the
    management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:


      a. as Chairman of the MSWMB,  designate a department and/or individual
         who will take the lead in the implementation and monitoring of  the
         solid waste management programs  and  activities  and  require  the
         immediate (i) submission of lacking documents of the Municipality’s
         10-year SWM plan  in  compliance  with  the  final  evaluation  and
         approval of the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC);
         and (ii) revision of the contents of the MSWMP consistent with  the
         NSWM framework;


      b. require the members of the MSWMB to exert effort in the performance
         of their duties and  responsibilities  and  implementation  of  the
         MSWMP  and  prescribed  policies  of  the  Ecological  Solid  Waste
         Management Act of 2000 to achieve the objectives of  the  ESWM  Act
         and ensure prompt delivery of basic services and facilities to  the
         community in relation to solid waste management  and  environmental
         protection;


      c. as soon as the budget allows, prioritize the creation of  Municipal
         Environmental and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) who shall  lead
         in the implementation of solid waste management programs and
         activities and monitoring thereof; and


      d. in coordination with the members  of  the  MSWMB,  fast  track  the
         processing of various requirements for the operation of a  sanitary
         landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. No. 9003 the
         soonest possible time to avert adverse environmental consequences.
