Engineering and Administrative Overhead to cover expenses for the preliminary survey, preparation of plans and program of works and another 3% under work item Project Supervision to cover expenses in the implementation of the projects until its completion were already capitalized as these work items are part and parcel in the realization of the project thus contributing to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management of the Municipality.

As per AAPSI for CY 2018 submitted by the Municipality, Subsidiary Ledgers/ Schedule for the 20% development fund is maintained and submitted to the Auditor every time it is required to be submitted.
observations.reason:
PPAs of 20% Development Fund was not optimally utilized due to road right of way problems.

Required detailed engineering activities have not been sufficiently carried out and adequate planning been done before these projects were proposed to be included in the list of the 20% priority projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a.	Furnish to the Audit Team a copy of MOA/Trust Agreement and other pertinent documents that would validate the transfer, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

b.	Adhere with the accounting policy of recording in the Trust Fund Books funds transferred to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
According to the DILG, LGUs which are Good Financial Housekeeping (GFH) passers are automatically entitled to receive funds from the DILG.  General guidelines relative to the transfer of funds will apply.  The former Audit Team also assured that ADA-2018-Tr4B-0035 indicating he particulars of the project was submitted instead.

For CY 2019, only the loan proceeds for ARCP was received by the Municipality which was duly recorded in the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality submit the Solid Waste Management Plan to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and approval by the National Solid Waste Commission so as to avail of the needed fund from the NSWC which could aid in the proper operation of the LGU’s sanitary land fill.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality, in CY 2018 failed to submit the ten Year SWM Plan. Nevertheless, the Focal Person is currently implementing the “no segregation, no collection policy in the municipality.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower and technical knowledge of individuals designated to oversee and monitor compliance with the provisions of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9003.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that:

the Inventory Committee conduct the complete physical count of all PPE accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding reports can be submitted to the Audit Team; 

the Municipal Accountant reconcile the resulting balance ofthe physical count with the accounting records. In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made; and

the Municipal Treasurer to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor created an Inventory Committee through Executive Order No. 03 series of 2018 dated January 22, 2018.
observations.reason: The RPCPPE for CYs 2018 and 2019 were not yet submitted to the Audit Team as of this writing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to establish a clear cut guidelines, policies and procedures on the selection and grant of cash assistance aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014.




We recommended that the Municipal Mayor leave the determination of the type, kind and amount of cash assistance to be given to the individuals based on the evaluation of the MSWDO in compliance with the aforementioned guideline.




We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that grant of cash assistance are supported with the required documentary requirements prior to processing for payment. Submit the required lacking documents to prove the validity, regularity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipality observe the limit of granting cash assistance directly to the recipients as required in the afore-cited memorandum circular.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor requested DSWD Ely Famorcan to appeal to SangguniangBayan to prepare an ordinance/resolution on the selection and grant of cash assistance.



The Municipal Accountant had coordinated with the MSWDO relative to proper documentation and additional requirement needed.



Cash assistance granted in CY 2019 were already supported with required documents.



Pending the enactment of a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution, the Municipality has already pegged the amount of cash assistance to P5,000.00.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still waiting for the Local Sanggunian to pass a resolution establishing the clear cut guidelines, policies and procedures on the selection and grant of cash assistance aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere strictly to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Copies of Official Receipts to prove that bidders paid for the bidding documents were already submitted. Audit of procurement of infrastructure projects for CY 2019 shows that deficiencies noted such as non-posting of RFQ and Notice of Award in the PhilGEPS and failure to invite observers were already acted upon.
observations.reason: Audit of procurement of infrastructure projects for CY 2019 revealed that the BAC failed to strictly comply with some of the provisions of the 2016 revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 thus, similar observations were still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.





We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to facilitate proper planning and monitoring of DRRM activities and see to it that the DRRM fund is optimally utilized for its intended purpose.





We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere strictly to the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRM fund set forth in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2019, a LDRRMFIP was prepared by the MDRRMO although not in the prescribed format.
observations.reason: LDRRM Fund was still not optimally utilized by the Municipality thus, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all other concerned officials and employees adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 in planning and implementing programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Charging of expenses not related to development projects was already stopped.
observations.reason:
Same with no. 6 above, PPAs of 20% Development Fund was not optimally utilized due to road right of way problems.

In addition, required detailed engineering activities have not been sufficiently carried out and adequate planning been done before these projects were proposed to be included in the list of the 20% priority projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to observe timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget to DILG Regional Office for review and approval in compliance with PCW-DILG-DNM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.




We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) facilitate the updating of its existing GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are responsive to address the same.



We recommended that the GFPS strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under JMC No. 2013-01 and to conduct regular coordination with the implementing office to ensure that plans are undertaken within the target period. Likewise, proper identification and prioritization of PPAs that would effectively address gender issues and concerns be conducted and ensure its inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget to be implemented during the year.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, a GAD database was being maintained by the Municipality, the main purpose of which is to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.reason:
The GAD Plan & Budget for CY 2019 submitted by the GAD Focal person was not duly reviewed by the DILG and was not issued with a Certificate of Endorsement due to failure of the Municipality to observe the schedule in GAD planning and budgeting.

Also, actual expenditures for GAD activities registered a utilization rate of only 25.47% or P860,267.00 compared with the budgeted amount of P3,378,008.80.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of R.A. No. 9003 and also consider the other requirements of the NSWMC, and submit the same to the NSWMC for final approval.



We recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the current composition of the MSWMB and look for possible members that could qualify to complete the line up. 




We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the significant provisions of Republic Act 9003 relative to proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste. Ensure the implementation of segregation of solid waste at source. Enforce “no segregation, no collection” policy, and stop the operation on open dump site for its garbage disposal, instead, developa sanitary landfill as final garbage disposal site of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality, in CY 2018 failed to submit the ten Year SWM Plan. Nevertheless, the Focal Person is currently implementing the “no segregation, no collection policy in the municipality.
observations.reason:
The Municipality, due to lack of a properly-designated department and/or individual to oversee and monitor compliance with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 failed to secure the certificate of approval for its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) from the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) for CY 2019.

The MSWMB also admitted that they have no proper knowledge in the preparation of WACS which is one of the requirements of the NSWMC in the approval of their plan. According to them they will seek the assistance of SWMB for WACS training.

They also reasoned out that the Municipality has no budget for a sanitary landfill that’s why they are still using a controlled dumpsite.  Aside from that, they could not locate a suitable sanitary landfill that will match the minimum criteria for siting of sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

strictly observe the existing rules and regulations governing collections and deposits of government funds to avoid the possible loss and misuse of government funds. 

observe the proper issuance of official receipts. See to it that a set of accountable form is entirely used up before using/issuing another set of official receipt for proper accounting and monitoring.
observations.managementAction:
Analysis of 
Collections and Deposits for CY 2019 show that deposits are being done weekly.
observations.reason: Undeposited Collections of P120,183.45 and P52,515.60 under the General and Special Education Funds, respectively, were noted as of end of CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents to establish the validity of year-end obligations. Also, review and analyze accounts payable in prior years to determine the validity of claims and take-up the required adjustments, where necessary.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019, Obligation Requests duly signed by the Head of the Requesting Office and Municipal Budget Officer were submitted.
observations.reason: Other documents such as payrolls, disbursements vouchers and other relevant documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with paragraph e, Section 4 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 10 of the same Manual, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) for selected accounts were prepared and maintained by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower, the Municipal Accountant could not prepare and maintain SLs for all controlling accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above, and submit lacking documents to prove the contract’s validity, accuracy, regularity and propriety.
observations.managementAction: Audit of procurement of infrastructure projects for CY 2019 revealed that submitted Construction Safety and Health Program of the bidder was duly approved by the DOLE; observers were invited to enhance the transparency of the procurement process; Notices of Award were posted in the PhilGEPS; and Manpower and Equipment Utilization Schedule were in the prescribed form.
observations.reason: There was no record as to whether the lacking documents noted were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare immediately a list of unexpended PDAF balances which will serve as the basis in the preparation of disbursement vouchers for the refund of the amount to the Bureau of Treasury in compliance with COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 9-81, COA Circular No. 94-013, Executive Order No. 431 and the Supreme Court ruling issued on November 19, 2013.

We recommended that: 

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the fund transfer to Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines (VLMP) or any implementing agencies is supported with MOA before the release of the fund as required under 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated        June 14, 2012.

The Municipal Accountant records the fund transfer to VLMP as Other Receivables instead of Donation to conform with the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that:
The Chairman of the LSB justify the use of the SEF for traveling and registration expenses which are not among the allowable expenses chargeable against the SEF.

The Local School Board allocate the Fund and utilize strictly in accordance with Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 of the Department of Education, DBM and DILG to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommended that: 

The Municipality allocate in its Annual Budget amount equivalent to at least 1% of the IRA for LCPC related activities pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344.

The Municipal Mayor direct the focal person of LCPC to prepare the Work and Financial Plan as well as LDPC and LIPC so that all priority PPAs for children are allocated with appropriate funding and maximize the utilization of LCPC fund to identified PPAs enumerated in DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 to ensure that rights and welfare of children are well protected.
observations.reason: Annual Budget for CY 2018 was already effective before issuance of the audit findings. To be implemented in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Head of the Inventory Committee prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property for all unserviceable motor vehicles and heavy equipment and submit the same to the Appraisal Committee so that they could immediately conduct appraisal, determine the appraised value and prepare a report thereon.

After the appraised values are determined, the Inventory Committee immediately file the application for disposal of the unserviceable vehicles, thru the Municipal Mayor, through an IIRUP with the Office of the Audit Team Leader for inspection and determination whether the subject property is with value or without value pursuant to Article 444 of the Rules and Regulations Implementing the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that:

The Project Engineer strictly monitor the project implementation in order to meet the agreed contract time such that the intended recipients or beneficiaries could immediately avail of the benefits derived from such projects.

The Project Engineer also ensure that all winning contractors who would be issued notices of award shall comply with the requirement of Section 39.4 of the said IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer close or transfer the government funds deposited with the PNB to any authorized government depository bank pursuant to the conditions and requirements set forth under DOF Department Circular No. 01-2017 dated May 11, 2017 so that it could comply with the mandate for the sound and efficient management of the financial resources of the government.

We recommended that:

The Project Engineer strictly monitor the project implementation in order to meet the agreed contract time such that the intended recipients or beneficiaries could immediately avail of the benefits derived therefrom.

The Project Engineer also ensure that all winning contractors who will be issued notices of award shall comply with the requirement of Section 39.4 of the said IRR.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor and the MPD Coordinator immediately implement projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund to achieve the socio-economic and environmental management outcomes. 


The MDC involved in the planning process ensure that development projects to be included in the Annual Investment Plan are properly evaluated and adequately planned to fast track project implementation.

The MPD Coordinator prepare and submit not only the current year Report of the 20% component of the IRA Utilization but also the status of implementation of all projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund.


The Municipal Officials concerned provide justification or explanation for charging the Improvement of Guesthouse, Senior Citizen Building and Pan-ad Booth against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Government strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumpsites pursuant to Section 2, Rule XVIII of Republic Act 9003 until such time that a sanitary land fill is constructed.

The Municipal Government expedite the construction of landfill so it could comply with the requirements of R.A. 9003 in its ecological solid waste management.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer’s Office regularly reconcile their records regarding completed and on-going projects so that the affected accounts are accordingly adjusted and in order to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

It is also suggested that all departments of the Municipality involve in the project implementation must submit their monthly project status report for consolidation and monitoring. 

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor create monitoring team who will take charge in the consolidation of reports of completed and on-going projects, programs and activities of all implementing offices of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit documents as proof that the Municipality has generated monetary savings as a result of the suggestions, inventions, superior accomplishments and other personal efforts of the employees or group of employees.

Further, the Municipal Mayor explain the granting of cash advance for the achievement incentives prior to the date of resolution approving the latter.

The Calatava PRAISE Committee submit evaluation reports or any documentation recognizing the productivity, creativeness made or ethical behavior of employees or group of employees making them eligible for the PRAISE monetary awards and incentives.

The Municipal Accountant ensure that the amount of incentives to be granted shall not exceed 20% of the monetary savings generated pursuant to the policies of the CSC approved Calatrava PRAISE.

The Municipality submit specific legal basis for the grant of Cash Incentives. Otherwise, we recommend that the said incentives be refunded by the Municipal Government officials and employees for lack of specific legal authority.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adhere to the provisions of the DILG & DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 with regard to the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and must use the same for the development projects or program that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures to attain the primary purpose for which the fund was established.

We further recommended that the Municipality stop utilizing the fund especially for expenditure items not covered or not connected with the development projects pursuant to DILG & DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Government strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumpsites pursuant to Section 2, Rule XVIII of Republic Act 9003 until such time that a land fill is constructed.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Government expedite the construction of landfill so it could comply with the requirements of R.A. 9003 in its ecological solid waste management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to immediately implement projects funded out of the 20% Development Fund to achieve the socio-economic and environmental outcomes. 

The Municipal Development Council and all officials involve in program and project implementation adhere to the provisions of the DILG & DBM Joint Circular NO. 2011-1 with regard to the utilization of the 20% Development Fund.  They must ensure that all projects to be funded shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

The Municipal Mayor create additional positions and hire personnel for the Office of the Municipal Engineer who will assist the Office in the implementation of projects.

The existing Monitoring Committee conduct constant monitoring of project implementation to ascertain that the desired level of efficiency are achieved and the services and benefits expected from these projects are delivered to the constituents of the Municipality without further delay.

The Municipal Mayor limit the hiring of consultants to undertake only the work or activities which is highly technical in nature and must adhere strictly with the provisions of Republic Act 9184.

The Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) carefully review the basis used by the Municipality in the hiring of consultants and also adopt the latest rules and regulations.

The MPD Officer and the Municipal Engineer including officials involved in the project implementation adhere to the provisions of the DILG & DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 with regard to the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure that all projects to be funded shall partake the nature of investment and capital expenditures.

The Municipality Mayor manage the resources of the government through proper disposition.
The Project Engineer submit duly received distribution lists and proof that the materials were properly utilized for its intended purpose. Otherwise, he shall be held liable for materials distributed not duly accounted.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.
	
We recommend also that the Municipal Accountant establish the Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year based on Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card or on a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

Further, we recommend that the Accounting Office observe the proper accounting method and accounting entries in recognizing and recording real property taxes.

Management should explain the use of the SEF for expenses not in accordance with its purpose. Should justification be unacceptable, the paid expenses of ₱251,188.80 shall be disallowed.

Funds allocated for SEF should be budgeted and utilized strictly in accordance with Article 363(c) of the IRR of R.A. 7160 and the provisions of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A and 01-B series of 1998,2000 and 2001, respectively to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommend that the management comply with the guidelines on the grant of cash advance for intelligence fund as set forth in Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND.

We recommend also that the GAD Focal Person adhere to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 on the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget.

Further, we recommend that the GAD Focal Person submit the Annual GAD Plan and Budget to the agencies requiring them.

We recommend that the agency adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 regarding the documentary requirements for procurement. It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the attached supporting documents are complete before affixing his signature.

It is recommended that the Officer-in-charge of the Municipal Accountant’s Office should exert extra efforts and initiate action plan for the immediate submission of the bank reconciliation statements from as of January 31, 2010 to May 31, 2013 and the subsequent periods.

We recommend that tangible items acquired with estimated useful life of more than one year shall be recorded as inventories and outright expense upon issuance in accordance with Sec. 2.2.1 of COA Circular 2005-002 in order to reflect proper account classification in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: a) strictly observe the existing rules and regulations governing the collections and deposits of government funds to avoid the possible loss and misuse of government funds. b) observe the proper issuance of official receipts. See to it that a set of accountable forms is entirely used up before using/issuing another set of official receipt for proper accounting and monitoring.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that -
The Municipal Treasurer submit a copy of the computer-generated collectibles at the beginning of the year as basis of the Municipal Accountant for the yearly recognition of its RPT/SET Receivables and the corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income in the books pursuant to Section 20 of MNGAS, Volume I.
The Municipal Treasurer develop strategies and exert extra effort to collect long overdue RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that Municipal Treasurer:
Intensify collection of delinquent RPT/SET accounts so that the Municipality could generate additional funds for the implementation of priority projects.
Launch effective tax campaign and send notice of delinquency to all past due RPT/SET accounts.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant review the Due to NGAs account specifically transfer of funds with dormant balances.  Further, we recommend that any unutilized balances must be returned to the Source Agency or may be utilized for other purposes depending on the conditions stated in the Memorandum of Agreement.
In the event that the funding source agency could not be determined, the Municipal Accountant request for write off pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer release the RPT share of each barangay directly to the Barangay Treasurer in compliance with Section 271 (d) of Republic Act 7160 instead of transferring the amount to the Trust Fund before releasing the same to the respective barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all dormant accounts shown in the financial statements and support the balances with documentary evidence in order to ascertain their validity, existence and correctness. Further, immediately initiate specific courses of action if correction is necessary.  In the event that the dormant accounts are found to be nonexistent, the said accounts should be elevated to COA for authority/approval of dropping them from the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Municipal Engineer expedite the implementation of the two BuB projects with funding from the National Government Agencies so the constituents could fully enjoy the benefits that can be derived from these projects.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts pursuant to Item 3.20 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to verify the correctness and validity of recorded entries in the general ledger and bank statements on time.
Request the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the general ledger balances of Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current Accounts and Cash Book balances of each bank account at least quarterly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Bids and Awards Committee:
Direct the D.K. Jocson Construction to expedite and complete immediately the implementation of the 120 sanitary toilets funded by the BuB program from the National Government Agencies so the constituents could fully enjoy the benefits from said projects.
Instruct the BAC to evaluate properly the offer of the participating bidder before awarding the contract or PO especially if there is only one participant in the bidding and ensure that all the basic documentary requirements for all types of procurement through public bidding prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 are attached to the voucher.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly the completeness and appropriateness of the supporting documents attached to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the fabrication and installation of conventional sanitary toilets to its proper asset account instead of Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses account to conform to the Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to revert the unutilized LDRRMF-QRF for CYs 2011 and 2012 under the STF to the General Fund thru Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), which shall be made available for other social services upon subsequent enactment by the sanggunian pursuant to Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to update the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund and Investment Plan and maximize the utilization of the prior years’ LDRRMF as well as the current year’s fund so it could serve the purpose of equipping the Municipality against the adverse impact of hazards and related disasters.
Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify MDRRM Fund included in the account Due to LGU and ensure proper classification and charges to the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/ Municipal Accountant:
Demand immediate liquidation/settlement of unliquidated cash advances especially those granted December 31, 2011 and below considering that a final notice and demand to all accountable officers must have been issued pursuant to Item 6.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-04.
Send the bill to personnel working in other government agencies demanding liquidation/ settlement/ refund of their unliquidated cash advances. Make representation on this regard to the Head of the Agency concerned and its Accountant.
Conduct periodic evaluation and validation of all unliquidated cash advances.  If necessary, he may request for the write off of dormant cash advances following the procedures of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Refrain from granting cash advance to national agency personnel considering that in case of failure to liquidate cash advance after the prescribed period has elapsed the institution of the withholding of salary could not be effected because they are not employees of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipality Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Administrator of the Transport Terminal to resolve issues regarding said project and commence operations the soonest possible time to avoid further loss of opportunity to earn additional income.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant a) transfer the
unexpended/unobligated balance of the MDRRM Fund to Special Trust
Fund under the “Trust Liability – DRRM” account b) use the proper
account in accounting LDRRM transactions. Likewise, we recommended the
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to account all
transactions that affects LDRRMF to eliminate differentiating balances
appearing in the report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to comply with the requirement of the perpetual inventory
system wherein regular purchases shall be coursed thru the inventory
account and issuances thereof shall be recorded as they take place except
those purchased out of petty cash fund which shall be for immediate use and
not for stock. Such case shall be charged immediately to the appropriate
expense accounts.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall
regularly prepare and submit accurate and complete Summary of Supplies
and Materials Issue (SSMI) with supporting Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS)
pursuant to Section 121 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, to the Accounting
Unit for proper recording/posting of issuance in the books of accounts and
Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs).

On the other hand, we recommended that Municipal Accountant
prepare JEVs to adjust/record issuances on the affected accounts and
coordinate closely with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure reconciliation of
their inventory records, also in respect of physical inventory taking.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through its
Inventory Committee, conduct complete physical count of the
properties of the Municipality, reconcile the results of the count with
the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate
RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual
on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the
computation of the depreciation expense with a lapsing schedule so as
to ensure the accuracy of the amount provided and to monitor the
correct balance of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the GFPS strictly monitor the
implementation of GAD PPAs as required under JMC No 2013-01
and to conduct regular coordination with the implementing office to
ensure that plans are undertaken within the target period. Likewise,
proper identification and prioritization of PPAs that would effectively
address gender issues and concerns be conducted and ensure its
inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget to be implemented during the
year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that
the completeness of recording transactions charged to the GAD Fund
and that the GAD Focal Point Person coordinate with the Accounting
Department and reconcile the draft accomplishment report with the
accounting records before it is finalized for submission to offices
concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, observe the
provisions given in allowing and disallowing the travel expenses claimed by
the officials and employees of the Municipality and see to it that
disbursement vouchers are completely supported with valid documents
before processing the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all other
concerned officials and employees adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-
DBM JMC 2017-1 in planning and implementing
programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the validity and completeness of the supporting documents attached to the payrolls before granting cash advance for payment of wages. Require the attachment of approved individual accomplishment report to payroll prior to approval of payment thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to establish a clear cut guidelines, policies and procedures on the selection and grant of cash assistance aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor leave the determination of the type, kind and amount of cash assistance to be given to the individuals based on the evaluation of the MSWDO in compliance with the aforementioned guideline.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that grant of cash assistance are supported with the required documentary requirements prior to processing for payment. Submit the required lacking documents to prove the validity, regularity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipality observe the limit of granting cash assistance directly to the recipients as required in the afore-cited memorandum circular.

We recommended that the Government’s Share for HDMF contributions of LGU employees be restored back to ₱100.00 per month in compliance with RA 9679 otherwise known as HDMF Law of 2009. However, the employees may continue to contribute more than ₱100.00 provided that their Net Take Home Pay shall not be less than ₱4,000.00.

We recommended that the Management submit a written explanation relative to the deficiencies noted above. We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee adhere strictly to the provisions of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184 in all stages of procurement.

We recommended that the management submit the necessary supporting documents for evaluation and ensure that all succeeding disbursements relative to the implementation of infrastructure projects by administration are properly supported with necessary documents to ascertain its compliance with the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the submission of the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to facilitate proper planning and monitoring of DRRM activities and see to it that the DRRM fund is optimally utilized for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere strictly to the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRM fund set forth in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all other concerned officials and employees adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 in planning and implementing programs/projects/activities funded by 20% Development Fund and see to it that development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to observe timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget to DILG Regional Office for review and approval in compliance with PCW-DILG-DNM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) facilitate the updating of its existing GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are responsive to address the same.

We recommended that the GFPS strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under JMC No. 2013-01 and to conduct regular coordination with the implementing office to ensure that plans are undertaken within the target period. Likewise, proper identification and prioritization of PPAs that would effectively address gender issues and concerns be conducted and ensure its inclusion in the GAD Plan and Budget to be implemented during the year.

We recommended that the Management revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of R.A. No. 9003 and also consider the other requirements of the NSWMC, and submit the same to the NSWMC for final approval.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the current composition of the MSWMB and look for possible members that could qualify to complete the line up.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the significant provisions of Republic Act 9003 relative to proper segregation, collection, transport, storage, treatment and disposal of solid waste. Ensure the implementation of segregation of solid waste at source. Enforce “no segregation, no collection” policy, and stop the operation on open dump site for its garbage disposal, instead, develop a sanitary landfill as final garbage disposal site of the LGU.

We recommended that the Memorandum of Agreement for fund transfer to barangay includes provision/obligation of the recipient LGUs to submit liquidation reports after the purpose of the fund transfer has been served.

The proponent barangay prepares the Project Proposal for approval of the Municipal Mayor. Once it is approved, the Municipality will then prepare the MOA.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant records the fund transfer to barangay as Due from LGUs instead of Subsidy to LGUs to conform to the Revised Chart of Accounts. In addition, he must require submission of liquidation reports for the financial assistance given or require the return or refund of the unexpended or unutilized amount pursuant to 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012 and ensure that previous fund transfer is liquidated after the purpose has been accomplished.

The Monitoring Committee conduct constant monitoring of project implementation to ascertain that the funds granted as financial assistance is utilized for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the management justify the use of the SEF for expenses not in accordance with its primary purpose. Should justification be unacceptable, the paid expenses of ₱350,632.26 be disallowed

The Local School Board allocate the Fund SEF and utilized strictly in accordance with Article 363(c) of the IRR of R.A. 7160 and the provisions of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A and 01-B series of 1998,2000 and 2001, respectively to attain the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommended that the Vice Mayor and members of the Sangguniang Bayan refrain from implementing programs and projects because it is not within their powers and mandated functions under Section 445 (a) and 447 (5) of the Local Government Code or R.A. 7160. 

The Local Chief Executive or the Municipal Mayor implement the programs and projects under the 20% Development Fund pursuant to Item 5.0 of Joint Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the LDRRM Officer refrain from utilizing the LDRRMF for the payments of expenses which do not support disaster risk reduction management activities and must be guided by the provision of    RA No.10121 and Item 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in the utilization of the Mitigation Fund.

Further, it is reminded that failure to comply with the submission of reports as cited in Section 122 of PD 1445 will automatically cause the suspension of payment of salaries of concerned officials or shall be punished under Section 128 of PD 1445.
